sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only row 63 changed, and it's the bottom border (timer/step counter). The white cells shifted by 1 position. This is just the HUD/timer changing, not a gameplay change. So clicking on the yellow square didn't do anything meaningful.
Let me reconsider the game. The key observations are:
1. The green square can be moved with arrow keys within the gray box
2. The blue and red objects can be toggled by clicking on the right side targets
3. The right side targets have white borders that disappear when clicked
4. The level is not complete yet
Maybe the goal is to move the green square to specific positions to "paint" or "activate" certain cells. Or maybe the goal is to match a pattern.
Let me try a different approach. Let me look at the game as a "painting" puzzle where the green square is a brush. When I move the green square, it might paint cells in its path. Let me check if the gray box interior changes as the green square moves.
Actually, I already checked this earlier. The green square doesn't leave a trail; it just moves. The cells where it was are now gray (gg), not green (NN).
Let me try another approach. Maybe the game is about moving the green square to specific positions to "collect" or "interact" with other objects. Let me check if the green square can leave the gray box.
Actually, let me try moving the green square to the top-left corner of the gray box and see if anything happens.
Wait, I should focus on the game goal. The "59 target actions" suggests that the game requires many actions. Let me think about what kind of game would require 59 actions.
If the green square can be at 9 positions (3x3 grid), and I need to visit all 9 positions, that's 8 moves (starting from one position). But 59 actions is much more than 9 positions.
Maybe the game involves moving the green square around many times, toggling objects many times, or both.
Let me try a completely different approach. Let me look at the game as a "logic puzzle" where the goal is to arrange the left side objects to match the right side pattern.
Actually, let me try to understand the game by looking at the right side targets. The red and blue cross shapes might be the target configuration for the left side. The white borders indicate "active" targets, and clicking them "completes" the target.
But the level is not complete yet. So maybe I need to do something else after clicking on the targets.
Let me try moving the green square to different positions and see if it triggers any changes. Maybe the green square needs to be at a specific position to "activate" the level completion.
Actually, let me try a systematic approach. Let me move the green square to all 9 positions and see if anything special happens at any position.
The 9 positions are:
- (38-39, 8-9), (38-39, 10-11), (38-39, 12-13)
- (40-41, 8-9), (40-41, 10-11), (40-41, 12-13)
- (42-43, 8-9), (42-43, 10-11), (42-43, 12-13)
Current position: (42-43, 12-13) (bottom-right)
Let me move to (42-43, 8-9) (bottom-left) and see if anything happens.
